price_details: Their site just says they specialize in weddings, elopements, and social events with no rates listed, so pricing is on inquiry.
notes: Went looking for K2 Event Co. after a friend mentioned them for a Telluride wedding, and this reads more like a full planning team than a photographer specifically. A 2025 review describes a tented celebration on Last Dollar Road for almost 200 guests that the team, led by Kristen, made feel effortless. Another reviewer called Kristen and Annie essential to their planning and rehearsal dinner process, though they noted some wires got crossed on the welcome party logistics. Worth a call if you want someone steering the whole day, not just camera coverage.

price_text: $7,400 average for full weddings, intimate packages from lower
price_details: Site lists a Denver intimate wedding/elopement option (up to 40 guests, 4 hours, add on hours) plus a Classic full storytelling package around $7,400 for 8 hours (up to 10). Full weekend coverage with rehearsal dinner and a 1 hour adventure session starts at $10K. Engagement session is 50% off when bundled with the Classic package. Add ons include film photography, photobooth, videography, and a vinyl DJ. No travel fee for Denver or DC, elsewhere is quoted case by case.
notes: - Style: 'Full Storytelling' with candid and editorial moments, all packages customizable - Also does courthouse and couple only elopements plus proposal photography - Reviewer highlight: called her 'the most talented photographer in the Denver metro area' and praised her direction when posing people - Another review flagged how personal a decision picking a wedding photographer is since you spend all day together, and said they're glad they picked her - Engagement sessions lean playful, taco trucks and noodle spots instead of the usual park backdrop

price_text: full day weekend packages start at $4k for 6 hours
price_details: weekday weddings get hourly rates instead of a flat package. engagement sessions come free with any wedding package, and if you book a standalone engagement session first that fee gets deducted from your wedding deposit later. couldn't get a read on travel costs so not mentioning those.
notes: ok so this one does photo AND video which is nice if you don't want to hire two separate people. she's based in durango but her site name-checks telluride and ouray for elopements too so she clearly travels the western slope for those. a review said she never made them feel rushed or like they were getting a contract shoved at them (shade at a previous photographer they'd talked to lol), and multiple reviewers said she helped with day-of logistics beyond just showing up with a camera. seems like a good option if you want someone more hands-on than just 'shows up, shoots, leaves.'

price_details: - Gold $5750 / Silver $5500 / Bronze $5250, all up to 8 hours of coverage - Hourly coverage available too, 4 hour minimum on weekends ($750/hr) - Elopements start at $3000 on weekends
notes: - Denver based, packages are all spelled out on the site up front which is refreshing vs the 'call for pricing' crowd - Review theme: FAST responder, one bride specifically called out how quickly she answered questions during planning - Booked for both engagement AND wedding photos per a couple who said they aren't 'camera people' but she made them comfortable - One reviewer got married at Blanc in Denver and said she handled their families well and was creative with shots

price_details: Three tiers on the site: Collection 1 at $2,000, Collection 2 at $3,000, Collection 3 at $3,600. No breakdown of hours or what's included in each tier beyond the price itself.
notes: -durango based, only 4 google reviews but all 5 stars -one reviewer specifically had an outdoor mountain wedding and said she worked hard to get a variety of shots and communicated well before/during/after -another reviewer compared notes with friends who paid more for other photographers and ended up disappointed, felt like she overdelivered for the price -small enough operation that there's not a ton of recent review volume to go on, worth asking for a fuller portfolio before booking

price_details: Their site doesn't list any rates, just describes covering proposals, engagements, intimate elopements, and destination weddings. You'd have to reach out directly for pricing.
notes: - Instagram: @katiefletcher_photography, based out of Monument - Review from 2019: 'practically invisible during the ceremony,' planners reportedly couldn't even find her right before the bride walked down the aisle - A more recent 2026 review says she's good at both big moments and small intimate details, and keeps communication clear throughout - Consistent theme across years of reviews is being unobtrusive during the actual ceremony while still catching everything

price_text: Quote only, no published wedding rates
price_details: Site says to schedule an initial call or video chat to go over pricing and details, so nothing's posted up front. Add-ons that ARE listed: a second photographer for $150/hour, an 8x8 album for $850 (10x10 for $1,100), and an engagement session for $450 (or $250 to upgrade the album). She delivers 50-75 photos per hour of shooting.
notes: Based in Durango but her reviews mention shooting a wedding out in New Jersey too, so she'll travel for the right booking. Reviews lean heavily toward family/portrait work (newborns, maternity, headshots) alongside weddings, and one reviewer specifically praised how she scoped out photo spots early at an unfamiliar venue before the ceremony started. You do have to go through the phone call step before getting real numbers, which could be a dealbreaker if you want to price-shop quickly.

price_details: Site doesn't post rates on the packages/investments page, just describes coverage windows: 4+ hours recommended for elopements, 6-8 hours for weddings.
notes: - Based in Crested Butte but says available everywhere, now booking 2026 and 2027 - Also does filmmaking, not just stills - Site leans hard into elopement planning specifically, offers to help scout locations and plan the actual day, not just show up and shoot - A 2026 review said she was somehow everywhere at once without feeling intrusive, and asked thoughtful prep questions about vision and timeline beforehand - Multiple reviews mention a Kara working alongside her, so bookings might come with a second shooter by default, worth confirming

price_details: couldn't get their pricing page to load so no rates to report, sourced entirely from her google reviews instead.
notes: she's based in the eagle river valley near vail and does engagement + wedding + family portrait work, one reviewer said this was their 4th project with her over the years. what stood out from the reviews: when a couple mentioned they were still deciding between multiple photographers, she reportedly didn't badmouth the competitor at all, just explained the style difference, which is a nice sign. another reviewer said she helped with posing and kept people laughing to ease nerves. no pricing info to go on though so you'd have to call.

price_details: couldn't get their pricing page to load so nothing on rates to share here.
notes: Denver based, does both weddings and elopements per her reviews (one couple planned an elopement with her from a different time zone entirely, which she apparently managed fine). Instagram is @leahgoetzel. The recurring word across three separate reviews is 'calming' or 'positive energy,' one bride said she was the first and only photographer they even spoke to because the first meeting sold them. No pricing info at all though, so budget-conscious couples would need to call before getting excited.

price_details: No numbers published, you email or fill out a form to get a custom proposal. What she DOES publish: a signed contract plus a 40% retainer locks your date, and packages include a second photographer/assistant option plus high-res print-ready files.
notes: - Telluride based, shoots at recognizable local spots by name: San Sophia Overlook, Gorrono Ranch, The Peaks Resort, Palmyra Lookout - Style is 'light and airy,' handles both intimate elopements and multi-day destination wedding weekends - Reviewer flew in from Atlanta for a Telluride destination wedding and called her one of the best decisions they made - Another reviewer specifically praised fast response times and said she stayed calm and accommodating even with last-minute schedule changes

price_details: Basic wedding day coverage starts at $2,200. Most couples land between $3,500 and $5,000 for their full collection. Comprehensive packages start at $2,750 with customized options for smaller weddings. Mini Wedding packages include up to 6 hours, 2 photographers, an online gallery, download/print rights, and a credit toward a signature album; the full package bumps that to up to 8 hours. Portrait sessions run from $550, event coverage is $275/hour, engagement sessions can be added at a discount.

price_text: Quote only, custom package pricing via consultation
price_details: Site doesn't post flat rates, instead walks through the booking process: get in touch, get a contract plus retainer request, sign and pay retainer to lock your date/time. Some concrete perks though: $50 off for returning clients, $100 in print credit if you refer someone who books a wedding or elopement, and a $50 discount if you both get to the session by bike or walking (an actual green discount). Travel within 30 miles of Denver is included in every package; further out is case by case.
notes: She shoots weddings, elopements, engagements, couples, boudoir, and lifestyle sessions. Turnaround varies a lot by shoot type: sneak peeks in 3 days, but full weddings and elopements take 4 to 6 weeks for the gallery and another 3 to 6 weeks if you want prints or albums. A wedding planner who's worked with her repeatedly called her reliably kind, timely, and professional, and specifically praised how well she communicates with other vendors on-site, which planners don't always say about photographers.
